mainEntity.name: Where can I buy a franchise?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can buy a franchise through three main channels: directly from a franchisor's corporate sales team, through a franchise consultant or broker network like Franchise Ki, or via franchise resale marketplaces such as BizBuySell. Franchise Ki recommends starting with a consultant for unbiased, AI-matched recommendations across 800+ brands.

mainEntity.name: What is the cheapest franchise to buy?

acceptedAnswer.text: The cheapest franchises start under $10,000 — typically home-based service brands in cleaning, lawn care, tutoring, and senior care. Examples include Jan-Pro, Cruise Planners, Dream Vacations, and Stratus Building Solutions. Always verify total investment in the FDD's Item 7, not just the franchise fee.

mainEntity.name: Should I buy a new franchise location or a resale?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on your goals. New territories offer prime markets and franchisor support; resales offer existing cash flow, customer base, and faster ROI. Franchise Ki evaluates both options against your specific financial profile during free consulting.

mainEntity.name: How do I verify a franchise opportunity is legitimate?

acceptedAnswer.text: Always request and read the Franchise Disclosure Document (FDD) before signing anything. Verify the franchisor is registered in your state. Speak with at least 5 current franchisees. Check Item 19 (financial performance), Item 20 (franchisee turnover), and Item 21 (financial statements). Franchise Ki performs this due diligence as part of every match.
